retry_reason: Failure occurs only on memory allocation or hash insertion error; these errors are typically not transient in a way that retrying immediately would succeed, and the caller should handle NULL as a permanent failure.
contract_summary: Get-or-create wrapper for batadv_orig_node. First attempts to find an existing orig_node via batadv_orig_hash_find (which acquires a reference). If not found, allocates a new node, initializes it, acquires an extra reference with kref_get for the hash table, and inserts it into the hash. On success, returns a pointer to the orig_node with a reference held for the caller. On failure (allocation or hash insert), releases all acquired references and returns NULL. The caller must check the return value and, upon successful return, must call batadv_orig_node_put() to release the reference when the node is no longer needed. The node remains accessible via the hash until it is removed separately.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to get-or-create an orig_node with proper reference count management. The function either returns an existing node (with refcount already incremented by the find callee) or creates a new node, initializes it, acquires an additional refcount via kref_get for the hash table, and adds it to the hash. Minimal non-refcount logic (spinlock init, hash add) is integral to setup.

check.reason: The function contains two get operations on the returned orig_node: one from batadv_orig_hash_find (returns with elevated refcount) and one from explicit kref_get after creation. Both increment the refcount of the object that is returned. The access path orig_node.refcount.refcount and type chain batadv_orig_node--kref--refcount_t are consistent with callee info and the code. The set and put operations are invalid: no refcount_set is present in this function, and the put operations act on a local variable only (return value is NULL), so they do not apply to the return value.
